page: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor built on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor configuration includes 16 physical cores with a single thread per core and a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores, falls within the average range. Memory performance is robust, with the PassMark memory score placing in the top quarter, while large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier.

The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, predictable multi-core execution, as CPU resources are shared with other tenants. However, the combination of top-tier single-threaded performance, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of local NVMe storage makes this instance suitable for general-purpose applications, database operations, static web serving, and file compression tasks that benefit from fast single-core burst capabilities and local SSD storage.
description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is a general-purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with a shared CPU allocation, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in single-core metrics, including stress-ng single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, reflecting the shared CPU resource model. This hardware profile provides a cost-effective balance of high single-threaded speed and substantial local storage, making it suitable for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose workloads that do not require dedicated, sustained multi-core CPU allocation.
